commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds SMS command authorization resolution, passes command access and text-slash command metadata into inbound SMS context, preserves `SenderE164`, and adds an allowlisted `/status` regression test.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +79. Total +108 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `main` builds SMS `CommandBody` but never passes `access.commands` or command facts into `buildContext`, while the PR's `/status` terminal proof exercises the intended positive path. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve the intended SMS `/status` fix while correcting the command-turn detector and adding one negative regression test.

target.title: fix(sms): authorize text slash commands
target.body:
## Summary

Native SMS direct messages that begin with text slash commands now carry command-turn metadata and command authorization into the inbound reply pipeline. This makes allowlisted/pairing-approved SMS senders follow the same command handoff shape used by other text-capable channels instead of leaving `/status`-style messages as ordinary chat text.

## Motivation

The native SMS channel from #88476 passes the inbound body through `textForCommands`, but SMS did not resolve command access or include command metadata when building the channel context. That left SMS behind channels such as Nostr, Slack, QQBot, and ClickClack, which already pass text command authorization into their inbound command paths.

Related: #88476 and #88601.

## Root Cause

SMS only resolved sender access with `resolveStableChannelMessageIngress`. It did not ask the channel command runtime whether the raw body should compute command authorization, did not pass the command options into ingress resolution, and did not set `access.commands` or text-command metadata in `buildContext`.

## Changes

- Include `channelRuntime.commands` in the SMS inbound runtime contract.
- Resolve SMS text-command access when the raw body is command-shaped.
- Pass `access.commands.authorized` and `command: { kind: "text-slash", ... }` into the SMS channel context for command-shaped messages.
- Preserve the normalized sender number as `SenderE164` in SMS inbound context extras.
- Add a regression test proving an allowlisted `/status` SMS is marked as an authorized text command turn.

## Non-goals / Scope Boundaries

- Does not change SMS pairing or sender allowlist behavior.
- Does not change command authorization policy globally.
- Does not add a live Twilio/Gateway integration test in this PR.

## Maintainer Fit

This follows the existing text-command handoff pattern from sibling channels: compute whether command auth is needed, let stable channel ingress resolve command access, then pass command authorization into `buildContext`. The PR is limited to the native SMS extension and its nearby tests.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Native SMS direct-message bodies that start with `/` now enter the inbound pipeline as authorized text-command turns when the SMS sender is allowlisted/pairing-approved.
- Real environment tested: Fresh local OpenClaw checkout based on current upstream `main`, running the patched native SMS inbound module directly through Node/tsx in the PR worktree. No live Twilio webhook, Gateway restart, or carrier SMS send was performed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR worktree, ran a Node/tsx terminal proof that imports `extensions/sms/src/inbound.ts`, dispatches an allowlisted `/status` SMS through `dispatchSmsInboundEvent`, and prints the context object received by `buildContext`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the patched module proof:

```json
{
  "context": {
    "message": {
      "rawBody": "/status",
      "commandBody": "/status",
      "bodyForAgent": "/status"
    },
    "access": {
      "commands": {
        "authorized": true
      }
    },
    "command": {
      "kind": "text-slash",
      "body": "/status",
      "authorized": true
    },
    "extra": {
      "MessageSid": "SM-command-proof",
      "SenderE164": "+15551234567",
      "To": "+15557654321"
    }
  },
  "routeSessionKey": "agent:main:sms:direct:+15551234567",
  "storePath": "/tmp/openclaw-sessions"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The SMS inbound context includes `message.commandBody="/status"`, `access.commands.authorized=true`, `command.kind="text-slash"`, `command.authorized=true`, and the normalized `SenderE164` value. That is the command-context handoff SMS previously lacked.
- What was not tested: Live Twilio/Gateway SMS behavior and carrier delivery were not tested in this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof intentionally avoids real webhook/config/runtime changes. The live carrier path should be proven separately after maintainers decide the code shape is acceptable and an operator has approved a live SMS/Gateway proof.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Source/read-only investigation showed SMS previously built `commandBody` but did not pass `command` or `access.commands` into `buildContext`, so final context normalized command authorization to false.

## Release Note Context

Fixes native SMS text slash-command routing so allowlisted/pairing-approved SMS senders can invoke text Gateway commands instead of having `/status` treated as normal chat text.

## Testing

- [x] `pnpm test:extension sms` — 10 files, 57 tests passed
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] `pnpm format:check -- extensions/sms/src/inbound.ts extensions/sms/src/inbound.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/sms/src/inbound.ts extensions/sms/src/inbound.test.ts --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json`
- [x] `pnpm lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-internal`
- [ ] `pnpm check:changed` — blocked locally by Testbox/crabbox sanity-check failure before project checks executed

## AI Assistance

This PR was prepared with AI assistance and reviewed before submission.
target.author: clawsean

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
